app.title: AB Test Research Designer
app.tagline: Plan the sample size and runtime for your A/B test, see which results you can trust, and export a clear decision — right in your browser.

llm.tokenRequiredFallback: Token required - falling back to local suggestions

wizardReview.fixFields: Fix these fields before saving or running analysis

wizardDraft.fixFields: Fix these fields before saving or running analysis

analysisFramework.title: Analysis framework
analysisFramework.description: Choose whether planning uses classic NHST power or Bayesian precision.
analysisFramework.frequentistDescription: Set alpha and power for the classic fixed-horizon approach.
analysisFramework.bayesianDescription: Set desired precision and credibility for interval-based planning.

helpText.traffic_split: Relative traffic weights per variant. Example: 50,50 for a balanced A/B split or 34,33,33 for three variants.
helpText.expected_daily_traffic: Average number of users or sessions entering the experiment each day. Example: 12000.
helpText.audience_share_in_test: Share of daily traffic that is eligible for the test. Use 0.6 for 60% of incoming traffic.
helpText.variants_count: Total number of variants including control. 2 = a classic A/B test, while 3+ variants increase sample needs.
helpText.baseline_value: Current baseline metric. For binary metrics use 0.042 for 4.2%; for continuous metrics use the current mean, such as 45.20.
helpText.expected_uplift_pct: Expected relative improvement versus baseline. Example: 8 means the treatment is expected to improve the metric by 8%. This is planning context only and does not change the required sample size.
helpText.mde_pct: Minimum detectable effect as a percent of baseline. Smaller MDE values require larger sample sizes. Sample size and duration are driven by this MDE, not by the expected uplift.
helpText.alpha: False-positive threshold. 0.05 means accepting a 5% probability of a Type I error.
helpText.power: Probability of detecting a real effect if it exists. 0.8 is the standard minimum for product experiments.
helpText.analysis_mode: Choose whether planning is framed with classic NHST power or Bayesian credible-interval precision.
helpText.desired_precision: Target half-width of the credible interval. For binary metrics use percentage points; for continuous metrics use raw units.
helpText.credibility: Credibility level for the Bayesian interval. 0.95 corresponds to a 95% credible interval.
helpText.std_dev: Standard deviation of the continuous metric. Required only for continuous metrics; example: 12.5 for average order value.
helpText.interference_risk: Risk that users or units can influence one another across variants and break isolation.
helpText.holdout_fraction: Global holdout: the share of traffic kept out of every experiment as a clean control. Leave empty for none, or enter 0.1 for a 10% holdout. It reduces the traffic this test receives, so the duration grows while the sample size stays the same.
helpText.mutually_exclusive_experiments: Number of mutually-exclusive experiments sharing the same traffic. Leave empty for 1. With N, this test receives 1/N of the traffic, extending the duration accordingly.
helpText.planned_test: Analysis method the sample size is planned for. The z-test plan is the classic default; Fisher's exact needs slightly more users on small samples; Mann-Whitney (rank test, for skewed metrics) inflates the plan by ~5%; equivalence (TOST) plans to CONFIRM the arms are the same within a margin instead of detecting a difference.
helpText.equivalence_margin_pct: Symmetric equivalence margin as a percent of baseline: the largest difference still treated as 'practically the same'. Drives the TOST sample size (the MDE does not apply to an equivalence plan).
helpText.avg_cluster_size: Cluster designs only: the average number of individuals per cluster (m), e.g. 100 shoppers per store. Drives the design effect DEFF = 1 + (m - 1) x ICC that inflates the required sample size.
helpText.icc: Cluster designs only: the intraclass correlation coefficient (ICC, 0-1) - how similar individuals in the same cluster are. Small in web/product experiments (0.01-0.05), larger in geo/classroom trials. 0 = no clustering penalty.

interimAnalyses.title: Interim analyses
interimAnalyses.tooltip: Number of times you plan to check results during the experiment. 1 keeps the standard fixed-horizon test. 2-5 enables O'Brien-Fleming group sequential monitoring with early stopping boundaries.

cuped.title: CUPED variance reduction
cuped.description: Use correlated pre-experiment behavior to reduce variance and compare naive versus CUPED-adjusted sample size.
cuped.enableTooltip: If you have pre-experiment data correlated with the outcome metric, CUPED can reduce required sample size by rho squared.
cuped.preExperimentStdDevTooltip: Standard deviation of the pre-experiment covariate, such as revenue in the week before the test.
cuped.correlationWithOutcomeTooltip: Pearson correlation between the pre-experiment covariate and the experiment outcome. Typical values are 0.3 to 0.7.

guardrails.title: Guardrail metrics
guardrails.description: Metrics to monitor without changing primary sample size. Up to 3 guardrails are supported.

validation.trafficSplitPositive: Traffic split must contain at least two positive weights.
validation.trafficSplitLength: Traffic split length must match variants count.
validation.expectedDailyTrafficPositive: Expected daily traffic must be greater than 0.
validation.audienceShareRange: Audience share in test must be between 0 and 1.
validation.variantsCountRange: Variants count must be an integer between 2 and 10.
validation.binaryBaselineRange: Binary baseline value must be between 0 and 1.
validation.continuousBaselinePositive: Continuous baseline value must be greater than 0.
validation.continuousStdPositive: Continuous metrics require a positive std dev.
validation.cupedStdPositive: CUPED pre-experiment std dev must be positive.
validation.cupedCorrelationRange: CUPED correlation must be between -1 and 1.
validation.desiredPrecisionPositive: Desired precision must be greater than 0 in Bayesian mode.
validation.guardrailBinaryBaselineRange: Binary guardrails require a baseline % between 0 and 100.
validation.guardrailContinuousFields: Continuous guardrails require baseline mean and positive std dev.
validation.guardrailMetricType: Guardrail metric type must be either binary or continuous.
validation.metricType: Metric type must be either binary or continuous.
validation.holdoutFractionRange: Holdout fraction must be between 0 and 1.
validation.mutuallyExclusiveMin: Mutually exclusive experiments must be an integer of at least 1.

sequentialDesign.unavailable: Sequential boundaries are not configured for the current analysis.
sequentialDesign.title: Group sequential design (O'Brien-Fleming)
sequentialDesign.footer: Stop early at any planned look if the observed absolute Z-statistic crosses the boundary. Otherwise continue to the next analysis.

srmCheck.title: Did traffic split as planned?
srmCheck.description: Enter observed user counts per variant to check for sample ratio mismatch. A red result means assignment or tracking may be broken.

multipleTesting.title: Correct for testing many metrics
multipleTesting.description: When an experiment tracks several metrics, testing each at the same level inflates false positives. Benjamini-Hochberg controls the false discovery rate (FDR); Holm controls the family-wise error rate (FWER). Enter each metric's p-value to see which stay significant after correction.
multipleTesting.needMetrics: Add at least one metric with a p-value between 0 and 1.
multipleTesting.summary: {{rejected}} of {{total}} metrics stay significant at level {{level}}.
multipleTesting.criticalValue: Rejects every metric with a p-value at or below {{value}}.

observedResults.title: Enter actual experiment results
observedResults.description: After the experiment finishes, enter observed data to compute statistical significance from actual outcomes.
observedResults.historicalReadOnly: Historical snapshots are read-only. Return to the live draft to save observed results.
observedResults.saveProjectFirst: Save the project first if you want to persist observed results.
observedResults.ratioDisclaimer: This plan's metric is a ratio. Analyze it post-hoc in the dedicated "Ratio metric (delta method)" section below — paste the raw per-user numerator and denominator values there; it runs the same delta-method z-test the live path uses. In this form you can only choose a conscious approximation: a continuous test on a precomputed per-user ratio value, or a Poisson rate test on the raw numerator events over denominator exposure.

fields.rawValuesPlaceholder: One value per line, or comma-separated

testType.label: Statistical test
testType.hint: Mann–Whitney compares whole distributions from raw values — robust to outliers and skew, with no normality assumption.
testType.fisherHint: Fisher's exact conditions on both margins and enumerates the hypergeometric distribution — an exact p-value for small samples or rare events, where the z-test's normal approximation is unreliable.
testType.boschlooHint: Boschloo's unconditional exact test treats only the group sizes as fixed and takes the supremum over the unknown shared success rate, ordering tables by Fisher's exact p. It is uniformly at least as powerful as Fisher's exact test — a stronger small-sample choice — at a higher compute cost, so it is capped to small tables.
testType.barnardHint: Barnard's unconditional exact test uses the same construction as Boschloo's but orders tables by a pooled Wald z-score instead of Fisher's exact p. Unlike Boschloo's, it does not uniformly dominate Fisher's exact test — prefer Boschloo's unless you specifically need Barnard's ordering. Same compute cost, so it is capped to small tables.
testType.rateHint: The Poisson rate test compares events over exposure (e.g. errors per 1000 sessions) with an exact conditional test. Use it for event-rate data where arrivals are independent — not for clustered per-user counts.
testType.bootstrapHint: The permutation test compares the difference in means by relabelling the pooled raw values — a distribution-free t-test — with a percentile bootstrap CI. No normality assumption; small samples are tested exactly.
testType.quantileHint: The quantile treatment effect compares a chosen quantile (median by default, or e.g. p90) between arms with a permutation test and a percentile bootstrap CI. Use it when a tail or the median matters more than the mean — skewed revenue, latency percentiles.
testType.trimmedTHint: The Yuen–Welch trimmed t-test compares means after trimming a fixed fraction from each tail, then uses a Winsorized-variance standard error and Welch degrees of freedom. Use it when heavy tails or outliers — revenue, order value, session length — would drag the ordinary mean and inflate its variance. trim = 0 reduces to Welch's t.
testType.equivalenceHint: The TOST equivalence test asks whether the difference stays within a tolerance ±margin in both directions — two one-sided t-tests. Use it to confirm no meaningful change (a refactor or migration that must not move the metric), where a non-significant difference test alone is not evidence of equivalence.
testType.ratioContinuousHint: Treats the ratio as a continuous per-user value (a precomputed numerator ÷ denominator) and runs the ordinary difference t-test on it — an approximation, not the delta-method test applied automatically in "Live experiment results".

validation.fillAllFields: Fill in all actual results fields with valid values before analysis.
validation.analyzeBeforeSaving: Analyze results before saving them to a project.
validation.saveUnavailable: Observed results could not be saved.
validation.savedToProject: Observed results were saved to project {{projectName}}.

chooser.description: Answer a few questions and we'll suggest a test. The full toggle above stays available.
chooser.incomplete: Answer the questions above to see a recommendation.

goal.label: What are you trying to show?

binarySmall.label: Are the counts small or events rare (any group < ~10)?

rationale.zTest: Large samples with adequate counts — the normal approximation is reliable.
rationale.fisher_exact: Small or rare-event counts — Fisher's exact avoids the unreliable normal approximation.
rationale.parametric: Roughly normal data, comparing means — the difference t-test fits.
rationale.mann_whitney: Small or non-normal samples — a distribution-free rank test avoids the normality assumption (bootstrap / permutation is another option).
rationale.trimmed_t: Heavy tails or outliers would drag the mean — trimmed means resist them (Mann–Whitney is a distribution-free alternative).
rationale.quantile: A specific percentile matters more than the average — compare that quantile directly.
rationale.equivalence: You want to confirm the metric stayed within a tolerance — that needs the TOST equivalence test, not a plain difference test.

cuped.title: CUPED-adjusted estimate
cuped.durationChange: Estimated duration changes from {{from}} to {{to}} days.
cuped.theta: CUPED coefficient theta = {{value}} (apply as Y' = Y - theta*(X - mean(X))).

planning.title: Planning
planning.caption: Design, sample size, and pre-launch checks

bandit.title: Bandit vs fixed split
bandit.description: Simulate how a Thompson-sampling bandit would shift traffic toward the best variant over time, and how much regret it saves versus an even split. Planning only — no live allocation.
bandit.binaryOnly: Bandit simulation is available for binary (conversion) metrics only.
bandit.probabilityBest: Picks the best variant {{percent}} of the time
bandit.regretReduction: Regret reduced by {{percent}} vs an even split
bandit.regretTitle: Cumulative regret: bandit vs even split
bandit.chartAria: Cumulative regret over {{steps}} steps, Thompson sampling versus an even split

assignment.title: Assign a user
assignment.description: Deterministically assign a user to a variation for this saved experiment, using the same GrowthBook-compatible hashing as your client SDK. The same user always lands in the same variation.
assignment.saveFirst: Save this experiment first to assign users to variations.
assignment.holdout: Held out — this user is not in the experiment (holdout / ramp-up tail).
assignment.growthbookNote: Compatible with GrowthBook SDKs — reuse the same key, seed and hash version client-side.
assignment.stickyNote: Sticky: this user already has a recorded exposure, so they keep this variation even if the traffic split changed.
assignment.namespaceExcluded: Mutually excluded — this user falls outside this experiment's namespace slot (not the same as a holdout).
assignment.attributesInvalid: Attributes must be a valid JSON object.
assignment.targetingExcluded: Not eligible — this user does not match the experiment's targeting rules.

liveStats.title: Live experiment results
liveStats.description: Recompute live statistics on demand over the deduplicated exposures and conversions collected for this saved experiment.
liveStats.saveFirst: Save this experiment first to read its live results.
liveStats.noData: No exposures recorded yet. Send exposure and conversion events to this experiment to see live results.
liveStats.comparisonPending: Not enough data for a test yet.
liveStats.sequentialCrossed: Crosses the sequential boundary — safe to stop early.
liveStats.sequentialNotCrossed: Below the sequential boundary — keep collecting data.
liveStats.alwaysValidHint: Stays valid under continuous monitoring (mSPRT) — safe to check anytime and stop as soon as the sequence excludes 0.
liveStats.sequentialActive: Information {{fraction}} of plan · boundary z {{boundary}} · planned {{planned}}/variant
liveStats.fixedHorizonProgress: Fixed-horizon plan: {{fraction}} of the planned sample collected ({{planned}}/variant).
liveStats.cupedCoverage: {{covariate}} of {{exposed}} exposed users have a pre-period covariate
liveStats.cupedComparisonPending: Not enough covariate data for a CUPED test yet.
liveStats.stratifiedCoverage: {{stratified}} of {{exposed}} exposed users carry a stratum · {{strata}} strata
liveStats.stratifiedPending: No stratum has both arms with enough users for a post-stratified test yet.
liveStats.guardrailPending: Not enough guardrail data for a breach test yet.
liveStats.eventTimingSummary: {{late}} late · {{outOfOrder}} out-of-order of {{total}} conversions (attribution window {{horizon}} d)
liveStats.eventTimingHint: Conversions arriving after the attribution window (late) or before the exposure (out-of-order), by event time — a data-quality signal; they don't change the result.
liveStats.identitySummary: {{linked}} identities linked · {{merged}} users merged · {{events}} events re-attributed
liveStats.identityHint: Anonymous events are folded onto each user's canonical (logged-in) id, so a person seen before and after login is counted once — preventing double-counted exposures and conversions.
liveStats.exclusionHint: Bot / fraud filter: users on the manual deny-list and users with an implausible number of conversion events are removed from every aggregate. The raw events are kept — only the analysis changes.

decision.title: Decision readout
decision.description: One synthesized verdict — ship, don't ship, or keep running — assembled from the live SRM, frequentist, Bayesian, and sequential signals. No new statistics.
decision.saveFirst: Save this experiment first to synthesize a decision.

verdictHint.ship: A treatment shows a confirmed, positive win over control.
verdictHint.no_ship: No treatment is a trustworthy improvement over control.
verdictHint.keep_running: The result is not conclusive yet — collect more data before deciding.

reason.no_exposures: No exposures recorded yet — start sending traffic to the experiment.
reason.insufficient_per_arm: Not enough users per arm yet to run a test.
reason.blocked_untrustworthy: Results can't be trusted until the blocker above is resolved.
reason.significant_win: {{arm}} is a significant winner ({{effect}} relative effect, p={{p}}).
reason.sequential_crossed: {{arm}} has crossed the sequential monitoring boundary.
reason.anytime_valid_confirmed: {{arm}} is confirmed by the anytime-valid view, which stays correct on early reads.
reason.sequential_not_crossed: {{arm}} looks significant but has not crossed the sequential boundary yet — keep collecting.
reason.fixed_horizon_before_planned_read: {{arm}} looks significant, but only {{fraction}} of the planned fixed-horizon sample is collected and the anytime-valid view does not confirm it — deciding now would be peeking; keep collecting.
reason.significant_loss: {{arm}} is significantly worse than control ({{effect}} relative effect).
reason.inconclusive_ci: {{arm}}: the confidence interval still includes zero — no clear effect yet.
reason.info_fraction_incomplete: Only {{fraction}} of the planned sample has been collected.
reason.info_fraction_complete_no_effect: The experiment reached its planned size without a detectable effect.
reason.guardrail_vetoed: A guardrail metric is breached, so the result is not shippable even if the primary metric wins.

blocker.srm_mismatch: Sample-ratio mismatch (p={{p}}) — assignment looks broken; fix randomization or tracking before trusting any result.
blocker.guardrail_breach: Guardrail "{{metric}}" is significantly degraded beyond its tolerance — shipping is blocked until the regression is understood.

categoricalResults.title: Categorical outcome (chi-square)
categoricalResults.description: Test whether a categorical outcome's distribution differs across groups with a chi-square test of independence on an r×c contingency table.
categoricalResults.tableHelp: One group per line (rows); counts per outcome level separated by commas or spaces (columns).
categoricalResults.lowExpectedWarning: Some expected cell counts are below 5, so the chi-square approximation may be unreliable. Consider Fisher's exact test for a small or sparse table.

validation.empty: Enter a contingency table (one group per line).
validation.parseError: Each row must contain the same number of non-negative whole numbers, with at least two rows and two columns.
validation.analysisUnavailable: Analysis is unavailable. Check the table and try again.

pairedResults.title: Paired samples (within-subject)
pairedResults.description: Compare two measurements of the same units — paired t-test, Wilcoxon signed-rank, or McNemar for paired binary outcomes.
pairedResults.valuesHelp: One value per line (or comma / space separated). Both arms must have the same number of values, paired by position.
pairedResults.mcnemarHelp: One value per pair, 0 or 1 (e.g. converted or not). Both arms must have the same number of values, paired by position.

validation.parseError: Enter at least two numeric values per arm (one per line or comma/space separated).
validation.lengthMismatch: Both arms must have the same number of paired values.
validation.analysisUnavailable: Analysis is unavailable. Check the values and try again.

ratioResults.title: Ratio metric (post-hoc)
ratioResults.description: Analyze a ratio metric R = Σnumerator / Σdenominator from raw per-user pairs — the delta-method z-test, the same math the live path uses.
ratioResults.valuesHelp: One value per user (one per line or comma / space separated). Within each arm the numerator and denominator lists must have the same number of values, paired by position — the pairing carries the within-user covariance the delta method needs.

validation.parseError: Enter at least two numeric values per field (one per line or comma/space separated).
validation.lengthMismatch: Within each arm, numerators and denominators must have the same number of values.
validation.analysisUnavailable: Analysis is unavailable. Check the values and try again.

omnibusResults.title: Multiple groups (omnibus)
omnibusResults.description: Compare a continuous metric across more than two groups (A/B/C/…) with an omnibus test — Welch's ANOVA (means, robust to unequal variances) or Kruskal–Wallis (distribution-free ranks) — before drilling into pairwise contrasts.
omnibusResults.groupsHelp: One group per line (at least two groups); values within a group separated by commas or spaces (at least two per group).

validation.parseError: Enter at least two groups, one per line, each with at least two numeric values (comma or space separated).
validation.analysisUnavailable: Analysis is unavailable. Check the values and try again.

survivalResults.title: Survival curves (log-rank family)
survivalResults.description: Compare time-to-event across two or more arms with Kaplan–Meier survival curves and the log-rank test family — unweighted (Mantel–Cox) or Fleming–Harrington weighted for early/late differences — for right-censored durations (churn, time-to-conversion, retention), where a censored subject is still event-free at last follow-up.
survivalResults.dataHelp: One subject per line: the follow-up duration, then 1 for an observed event or 0 for a censored (still event-free) observation. A line with only a duration counts as an observed event.

validation.parseError: Enter at least one subject per arm, one per line: a non-negative duration optionally followed by 1 (event) or 0 (censored).
validation.analysisUnavailable: Analysis is unavailable. Check the values and try again.

chart.ariaLabel: Kaplan–Meier survival curves for the control and treatment arms

survivalResults.fhHelp: Weights w(t) = S(t−)^ρ · (1−S(t−))^γ: ρ > 0 emphasizes early differences, γ > 0 late ones; ρ = γ = 0 is the plain log-rank.
